role: ASU Future of War Senior Fellow / former NSC Director for Iraq

recentTweets.text:
Yes, I'm running.  I'm proud to announce my candidacy for U.S. Congress in Virginia's 7th district.

https://t.co/duJNaz8fUE
https://t.co/oXtGPdYblK

@FischerKing64 In the 80s, I think you could go to any top-tier state university and "work your way through school" with a relatively mundane summer job and 10-15 hours/week of work/study.

This plan won't put much of a dent in tuition/room/board today.

This is exactly right.  As my boss at NSC once told me, “Our job is 10% making policy, 20% disseminating policy, and 70% beating those m*****f******s in the Departments into submission.”  

Too many (including some occupants) see the NSC as a grand strategy club.
@AlexHeffron20 Is someone asking the question, "What resources were provided to these applicants to enable these achievements at such a tender age?"
@azelin OK.  AQ splinter group.

Bolton joined the National Guard to avoid going to Vietnam. 

Tulsi joined the National Guard to get to Iraq.  

Draw your own conclusions.

@alx Let's back the tape up.  The NY Dept of Environmental Conservation.....has a Special Operations Unit?  Are there organized criminal poaching rings in NY of which I was previously unaware, that require special operations and tactics?

@TulsiGabbard My youngest son went through BCT at Sill.  I could talk all day about everything that is wrong with my beloved Army.  But we are still very good at taking young kids “off the street” and turning them into adults and Soldiers.  Thanks to you and your Drill Sergeants!!!
@jessesingal Writing and facts aside, a London-based Brit covering crime and police in Chicago for a major US outlet?
Baghdad, 20 years later. https://t.co/anoGN3G0FJ
@EmmaMAshford Is it an escalation if it’s been going on since (at least) spring?
@EmmaMAshford I’m shocked that you’re shocked.
@dr_seminnis @AnnaKellyWI I’ll stop double spacing after a sentence when Word stops courteously inserting a period when I do.
Coco was a great help with today’s mowing. https://t.co/gOU7Kc9Ewh

I’m taking great joy in planting resistant chestnut saplings (@nativnurseries) to replace these borer-killed white ash.  Can’t do anything about the ash.  But helping bring back chestnut is “full circle.”  

Thanks @cognazor and @aspiringpeasant for inspiration. https://t.co/ptPWHMRH3V
Remarkably insightful and well written on AUKUS, subs, France and China.
.⁦@Aelkus⁩ wins tweet of the week.  Maybe of the month. https://t.co/b6YaTFD9MK

Good to be back in Baghdad after almost two years.  

#Iraq https://t.co/yfzoM1PZEN

@ischinger @peterbakernyt By those who abandoned Ukraine for a little cheap gas?  

Spare me.
@WashProbs Bypassing tourists “standing left.”

RIP, Scott.  

A sad and tragic reminder that there will be Afghanistan fatalities among servicemembers long after this summer.
God forbid we keep China from throwing blood and treasure into this abyss.
So @charlie_simpson said I was having a good hair day. https://t.co/x2VUwF88Ib

Great to see ⁦@witticus10⁩ and @coffeeshopjihad⁩ at The Compound today.  

I’m happy to report that they are not quite so cloyingly cute in person as they are online. https://t.co/CgDoovwjdB
First of the year! https://t.co/gbhbBWGTt0

This is nonsense on stilts.  COOs make a half million annually because they know how to MAKE MONEY, which military officers do not.  

Wake me up when someone turns down command of a division or fleet because GE or IBM poached him/her.

https://t.co/OtQUZDAePL via @DefenseOne

@joeynolfi A lion in winter, tended by a young lioness--as powerful in her prime as he was in his.  Truly beautiful

Tony is virtually the last of the legends.  Almost all those he recorded with in his prime have preceded him.  We were fortunate to have him as long as we did.
@GraceSpelman Let me assure you that nothing could be more normal than not being able to get anyone in the Army on the phone.

Just got my first WhatsApp message from Baghdad asking if I'm okay in Washington (yes, Hayder, I'm fine; not even in DC).

You can't make this up.

@laraseligman @nahaltoosi Women in defense is much bigger than Flournoy.

I could easily suggest an all-women (all-Obama alum) team for the next tier at the Pentagon. 
Deputy SECDEF--Jane Lute
USD-Policy--Kim Field
USD-Intel--Jen Easterly
USD-A&S--Janine Davidson
(Elissa Slotkin has a job...)

Many more.
Very pleased to be joining this great team, led by Rollie and Aaron.
My first grandchild, a girl, born of the wife of my second son today.  No words.
Tweet of the year.

This will be the first year in some time I've not visited Chris on #Memorial Day, though I'm sure he'd understand.  But he's on my mind today.  Plus Paul, Todd, Laura, Drew.  Andy Weiss.  Mark and Jim.  Pablo and Jose.

Absent companions.

.@ericagaston and I explore proxy warfare in #Iraq and find that external powers may find that local interests trump that of outside patrons.  We project that this may be a larger trend.  Convergence of interests with a "proxy" may be key factor.

https://t.co/zfdky2KyHQ

My war experience was very different (and different each time) but this thread captures both essence and nuance far better then most.  

h/t @EvansRyan202

@Baherr__ Anyone who says they know how this plays out is a fool or a liar.  We are in totally uncharted territory.  I could craft narratives that say this is either victory or devastation for US interests with equal ease.

Supreme Court denies Adnan Syed a new hearing.  

In other news:

@rabiasquared 

https://t.co/LxsSqDPpNj

In which the UN SRSG, my friend Janine, arrives at the #Iraq demonstrations by Tuk Tuk.  With the local flag. 

Anyone doing public diplomacy should be taking copious notes.  

This is how you do it. https://t.co/SF9LzkypI7

@iAmTheWarax Indeed.  Al Baghdadi deserved to die for his role in launching a genocidal campaign on 2+ countries.  

But I’d also be perfectly fine had we tracked him down solely for what he did to Kayla Mueller.

@pptsapper .@Slate published a great piece a few years ago on a similar mission in 1871.  The narrative will "rhyme" to anyone who was in Baghdad 2004-7.

https://t.co/Z8O1iVKfh3

Typical @CrisisGroup excellence.  This report—deeply informed by field research—analyzes not only ISIS itself but the new environment in which it must operate.  Realistically assesses the ways in which ISIS might return, but accurately its current capabilities as “banditry.”

So @Observer46664 finally got me to Al-Reef cafe on 42d St.  I’m a little shocked by her devotion to this hipster venue, but the coffee is very good!  And always great to get her insights. #Iraq https://t.co/CvQwQ8fP1s

There are so many places in Baghdad where you can just feel the energy of a young, vivacious, optimistic population ready to take on the world.

This isn't one of them.

#Iraq https://t.co/Z0sXrfjZ9G

Question most asked of me today in Baghdad:
“Your new National Security Advisor isn’t another MEK crazy, is he?”
Great profile of a figure mostly unknown outside the Pentagon (and even some places there).
Thrilled to see this.  #Iraq
This is why I put on weight in #Iraq. https://t.co/xAgUrpAQ9k

Out at the Baghdad Mall today.  The sign says the @BurgerKing is "coming soon."  Inshallah....
#Iraq https://t.co/uNmVgE28SV

I was thrilled to return to Kadhamiya for the first time in over a decade tonight.  It was deeply satisfying to return to where my engagement with #Iraq first began over 15 years ago.  Being able to experience the peaceful bustle at the Imam Kadhim shrine was quite moving. https://t.co/ZrTn345Lrz
Good morning Baghdad. I’m still getting used to just cutting through the Green Zone to get from A to B. https://t.co/V1WMjRk4Lz
This tweet is a huge "tell" that you've never seen today's young Americans perform under fire.
So 5/7 done! (Only because she dared me to say that) Congratulations  @hopeollivant ! https://t.co/c0rsOZ1yof

I think it's worse than this, Ryan.  We now have an entire generation of military, DoS, and USAID officers who think/believe that whatever they were doing in Afghanistan was "strategy." How can we expect them to think rationally about another case?
A quiet Friday in Baghdad.  Shopping at Mansour mall, then Indian food with friends.  #Iraq https://t.co/DynEq0jpLw
In Baghdad. Taking Careem. Through (opened) Green Zone. Alone. On a Thursday night. Just to see friends.  It’s not 2007 anymore.

kurdistanMentions.text:
Bolton joined the National Guard to avoid going to Vietnam. 

Tulsi joined the National Guard to get to Iraq.  

Draw your own conclusions.
Baghdad, 20 years later. https://t.co/anoGN3G0FJ

Good to be back in Baghdad after almost two years.  

#Iraq https://t.co/yfzoM1PZEN

Just got my first WhatsApp message from Baghdad asking if I'm okay in Washington (yes, Hayder, I'm fine; not even in DC).

You can't make this up.

https://t.co/Z8O1iVKfh3

This isn't one of them.

#Iraq https://t.co/Z0sXrfjZ9G

Thrilled to see this.  #Iraq
This is why I put on weight in #Iraq. https://t.co/xAgUrpAQ9k

Out at the Baghdad Mall today.  The sign says the @BurgerKing is "coming soon."  Inshallah....
#Iraq https://t.co/uNmVgE28SV

I was thrilled to return to Kadhamiya for the first time in over a decade tonight.  It was deeply satisfying to return to where my engagement with #Iraq first began over 15 years ago.  Being able to experience the peaceful bustle at the Imam Kadhim shrine was quite moving. https://t.co/ZrTn345Lrz
Good morning Baghdad. I’m still getting used to just cutting through the Green Zone to get from A to B. https://t.co/V1WMjRk4Lz
A quiet Friday in Baghdad.  Shopping at Mansour mall, then Indian food with friends.  #Iraq https://t.co/DynEq0jpLw
In Baghdad. Taking Careem. Through (opened) Green Zone. Alone. On a Thursday night. Just to see friends.  It’s not 2007 anymore.
Good morning Baghdad.  I’ve missed you. https://t.co/4JmchokbQl

Far and away the most concise and accurate statement of general Iraqi opinion I've yet seen.  
American policymakers/commentators continue to talk about Iraq in terms we would NEVER use regarding another ally.
Great Indian food...in #Baghdad. https://t.co/Y7OSgdkGFA
When we start losing smart, observant, English-speaking good friends in #Iraq, think about how we must be doing with the illiterate young men in Dhi Qar.

Looking forward to @mideastmargaret's newly contracted book on #Iraq's elite counterintelligence unit The Falcons!  I think this will be an important look into the internal resistance against Daesh, and by a great journo with a long-term outlook.

https://t.co/ctq1ESEfAL
So let me get this straight. My country’s Senate is voting to condemn the withdrawal of troops from Syria, a deployment which it never voted to authorize?  I weep for Article One.

I do not have an informed opinion of the issue of converting MoE contractors to full time workers.  What I DO know is that having a #Iraq minister publicly explaining his actions and answering follow-ups, in Arabic and English, is a promise of long-overdue GoI transparency.
This are astounding numbers for those of us who lived through #Iraq’s dark days.

I am the only one slightly teary-eyed at this touching moment between the young Nobel Laureate and the distinguished Head of State--and that it's happening between two non-Arab Iraqi minorities here in Baghdad?  And Dr. Barham's message on her reception in perfect pitch.
Proud #Iraq out tonight. Sirens, lights,celebratory fire. Celebrating liberation from #ISIS. I’m so happy to be here. #closure.

In the “good problems” category, huge traffic issues in Baghdad this morning caused by removal of T-walls, preparing for test opening of Green Zone. Neighborhoods are looking across the street for the first time since 2003.
I left this meeting feeling much more optimistic about the future of #Iraq. And I like the new house! https://t.co/rCn58iKq7i
A lazy afternoon in Baghdad, drinking coffee at @thestationiq and talking to new friends and old.  So much better than the old days. #Iraq https://t.co/pDhhuRMgWv

The following is circulating on #Iran and #Iraq social media after PM Abadi indicates he will honor the US sanctions on Iran.  No need to translate the Farsi to get the meaning!!! https://t.co/NlJ6Pz5zcr
Enjoying dinner out in Baghdad. But it was hard to talk. There was some loud sporting event going on....  #Iraq https://t.co/7GbcqduoYz

A vivid depiction of the reality of democracy in #Iraq.  In what other state in the region would a citizen dare to shout accusations at the Head of Government without fear of arrest (or worse)?  I'll again quote my friend @ImaraWaTijara--Iraq has space for political dialogue. https://t.co/C1cGf7EWoy

Anecdote in this article says it all. Haider al-Abadi’s Victory list’s candidates are 30% Sunni, 10% minorities, and 5% Kurds.  This means Arab Shi’a, with 55% remainder, are numerically under-represented on a “Shi’a” list!  #Iraq #IraqElections2018 https://t.co/1Vfd1EWdhX
It was a pleasure to work with my friend @bb_bull on this report. We believe this summarizes what US-#Iraq relations both are and should be. https://t.co/ayCm3sPpJh

State Department deletes their old tweet and reissues a much more appropriate version that lets us focus on the inspiring heroism of Um Qusay against the genocide of #ISIS.  #Iraq https://t.co/c1mHYDBUlb

Hi I’m the State Department. I’d like that congratulate you on your incredible personal courage....but let me indulge in some ignorant insults of your country and society first. #facepalm #Iraq https://t.co/MuMjwgHszZ
I have known about Mutanabbi Street for years, but had never been.  It’s great having the security situation in Baghdad be such that I can (easily) go!  #Iraq @MantidIntlLLC https://t.co/fAjHJR8B22
.@peterbergencnn highlights LTG Saadi of the #Iraq CTS, the force that spearheaded the anti-#ISIS fight.  https://t.co/XChICo4x6p @NewAmerica
Important insight that Baghdad's most important weapon in the Kirkuk crisis was its democratic legitimacy, in contrast to the creeping authoritarianism in the KRG. #Iraq https://t.co/viG9bvi6vU

The end of #ISIS territorial rule in #Iraq. Congratulations to the Iraqi people and military. And we should all take a moment to remember those who gave their lives to make this happen. https://t.co/e4rJHH0kbF
The Iraqi army that fled in 2014 has little in common with the battle-hardened Iraqi Army (CTS-led) that liberated Ramadi/Fallujah/Mosul. https://t.co/kgHyTJFOPc
Bottom line. The region and its politics are changing. Don't be stuck in last decade's (or millennium's) paradigms. #Iraq #KSA https://t.co/c54bpxBEmj
Read thread, in which an American analyst explains Sunni Moslawi psychology....to a Sunni Moslawi. #Iraq https://t.co/ffi8wrfNg1

In which I help @StevenMufson think through some "minor" problems with Trump plan to seize #Iraq oil.
https://t.co/XTy00jQRtZ @NewAmerica
Sleeping peacefully in #Karbala tonight. It's not 2004 anymore. #Iraq http://t.co/xwjx0aembb

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 48 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Douglas A. Ollivant. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Iraq / Baghdad / Kirkuk, Oil / Energy, KRG / IKBY, Kurds / Kurdish.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 2 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 7 security-frame hits, 11 supportive or diplomatic hits, 1 humanitarian/minority hit, and 2 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2024-11-23T20:55:10Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

Happy 18th birthday to my daughter Hope, who can't be tagged in this tweet because she's wise and discreet enough to block her father on Twitter.
